questions.question: Fraunhofer diffraction occurs when the light source and screen are:
options: At infinite distance from the slit
questions.answer: At infinite distance from the slit
questions.explanation: Fraunhofer diffraction assumes parallel rays produced by effectively infinite source and screen distance.

questions.question: What causes diffraction of light at a slit?
questions.answer: Bending of light waves when passing through a narrow aperture.
questions.explanation: Diffraction occurs when the slit width is comparable to the wavelength.

questions.question: A diffraction grating consists of:
options: Large number of equally spaced slits
questions.answer: Large number of equally spaced slits
questions.explanation: Gratings produce sharp interference maxima.

questions.question: What does resolving power of a grating measure?
questions.answer: Ability to distinguish two close wavelengths.
questions.explanation: Higher resolving power allows better spectral separation.

questions.question: The grating equation is:
questions.explanation: This equation determines diffraction maxima.

questions.question: Stimulated emission is the principle behind:
questions.explanation: Laser amplification occurs through stimulated emission.

questions.question: What is population inversion?
questions.answer: More atoms in excited state than ground state.
questions.explanation: This condition is necessary for laser action.

questions.question: Metastable states in lasers help in:
questions.explanation: Longer lifetime helps population inversion.

questions.question: He-Ne laser emits light typically of wavelength:
questions.explanation: He-Ne laser emits red light.

questions.question: Name one application of lasers.
questions.explanation: Lasers are used in holography, surgery, and communication.

questions.question: Light propagates in optical fiber due to:
questions.explanation: Core-cladding structure enables internal reflection.

questions.question: What does numerical aperture represent?
questions.answer: Light gathering ability of the fiber.
questions.explanation: Higher NA means more light acceptance.

questions.question: Single mode fibers support:
questions.explanation: Single mode fiber reduces dispersion.

questions.question: Critical angle is defined for:
questions.explanation: It determines whether light reflects internally.

questions.question: Main advantage of fiber optic communication?
questions.answer: High bandwidth and low signal loss.
questions.explanation: Fiber allows long-distance data transmission.

questions.question: An example of a vector field is:
questions.explanation: Vector fields have magnitude and direction.

questions.question: Gauss law relates electric flux to:
questions.explanation: Flux equals charge divided by permittivity.

questions.question: State Faraday’s law of electromagnetic induction.
questions.answer: Change in magnetic flux induces emf.
questions.explanation: Basis of generators and transformers.

questions.question: Ampere's law relates magnetic field to:
questions.explanation: Magnetic field circulation depends on current.

questions.question: How many Maxwell equations describe electromagnetism?
questions.explanation: They unify electricity and magnetism.

questions.question: Special relativity was proposed by:
questions.explanation: Einstein proposed it in 1905.

questions.question: What is time dilation?
questions.answer: Time runs slower in moving frames.
questions.explanation: Observed at velocities near light speed.

questions.question: Length contraction occurs along:
questions.explanation: Observed length decreases along motion.

questions.question: The mass-energy relation is:
questions.explanation: Mass converts to energy.

questions.question: Nanotechnology deals with structures of size:
questions.explanation: Nanomaterials lie in nanometer scale.

questions.question: Why do nanoparticles show unique properties?
questions.explanation: Surface effects dominate at nanoscale.

questions.question: Which tool is used to image nanoparticles?
questions.explanation: Scanning Electron Microscope visualizes nanoscale surfaces.

questions.question: PT100 is a sensor used to measure:
questions.explanation: PT100 resistance varies with temperature.

questions.question: What principle do piezoelectric sensors use?
questions.answer: Generation of voltage when mechanical stress is applied.
questions.explanation: Piezoelectric materials convert mechanical energy to electrical energy.

questions.question: Photodiodes are used to detect:
questions.explanation: Photodiodes convert light into electrical current.
